      Transnational Party Organisation
      The TPO database maps how political parties organise and maintain connections beyond their national borders. It measures different forms of transnational party organisation, including physical infrastructure such as international branches, offices, and networks, organisational structures and rules, and digital outreach through social media platforms.
      The database works with different levels and indicators for each level. For example, it examines whether parties have official branches abroad, how formalised these organisations are, whether members abroad have rights within the party, and how parties communicate with citizens living overseas.
      It also looks at the activities of these networks, as well as their online presence on social media platforms. Overall, the database helps researchers and the broader public better understand how political parties operate across borders and engage with non-resident citizens.
    
    
      
        Score Dimension
          
            The dashboard distinguishes between the main dimensions used to classify transnational party organisation.
            
              Dashboard dimensions
              
                Infrastructure: physical transnational party infrastructure.
                Formalisation and Incorporation: organisational variation in transnational party infrastructure.
                Social media: digital transnational party infrastructure.
              
            
            
              Neutral view
              All dimensions shows the four measures together and supports cross-case comparison.
            
          
        
        
          
            Neutral view
              
                All dimensions
                Shows all four score dimensions together.
                
                  Filtering logic
                  When a Score Level is selected, parties match if they have that level in at least one dimension.
                
              
            
            
              All dimensions
            
          
          
            Physical infrastructure
              
                Infrastructure
                Physical transnational party infrastructure is ranked with five criteria.
                
                  Website dedicated to international or external-citizen activity.
                  Own international office.
                  Number of branches or contacts abroad.
                  Size, measured through members abroad where available.
                  Affiliated institute.
                
              
            
            
              Infrastructure
            
          
          
            Organisational variation
              
                Organisational variation captures how transnational party infrastructure is structured and linked to the national party.
                
                  Formalisation
                  
                    Statutory recognition.
                    Registration of branches.
                    Membership status of members abroad.
                    Branches with their own rules and procedures.
                    Type and regularity of activities.
                  
                
                
                  Incorporation
                  
                    Organisation initiated at home or abroad.
                    Organisation controlled by the home party.
                    Ideological linkage.
                    Formal rights within the national party.
                    Influence over domestic party policy.
                    Delegation in the national party organisation.
                    Financial support from the national party.
                  
                
              
            
            
              Formalisation
              Incorporation
            
          
          
            Digital infrastructure
              
                Social media
                Digital transnational party infrastructure captures dedicated expat or external-citizen channels on major social media platforms.
                
                  Presence on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter/X, or TikTok.
                  Audience size, measured through follower counts where available.
                  Recency of activity, measured through the last entry.
                
              
            
            
              Social media
            
          
        
        
          Score Level
            
              
                Infrastructure
                High international website or office and branches or contacts abroad.
                Medium international division, party branches, or external contacts.
                Low no organisational infrastructure.
              
              
                Formalisation
                High statutory recognition, formally registered branches, official members, institutionalised rules, and regular activities for citizens abroad.
                Low one or more of these conditions is not met.
              
              
                Incorporation
                High initiated and controlled by the home party, ideologically linked, formal rights for external members, national-party representation, and influence over policy.
                Low one or more of these conditions is not met.
              
              
                Social media
                High more than two dedicated expat channels.
                Medium one or two dedicated expat channels.
                Low no dedicated expat social-media channel.
              
            
          
          
            All
            Low
            Medium
            High

      Selection data
      Interactive selection summary
      This window summarises selected countries or regions with low, medium and high score aggregates per dimension. Select one party to see its four-dimension profile, or select up to five parties to compare their scores side by side.
